lines.line: # Introduction to the Oral History Interview

> The speaker introduces themselves as Asher Bruce, a student conducting an oral history interview with Tony Ferro, a political candidate and native of Treasure Valley, Idaho. The interview is part of a public history class project in collaboration with the Idaho State Historical Society and is supported by the NIH Spotlight on the Humanities Grant program. The date is April 12th, 2024, and the location is the College of Idaho in Caldwell.

metadata.title: Introduction to the Oral History Interview
metadata.synopsis: The speaker introduces themselves as Asher Bruce, a student conducting an oral history interview with Tony Ferro, a political candidate and native of Treasure Valley, Idaho. The interview is part of a public history class project in collaboration with the Idaho State Historical Society and is supported by the NIH Spotlight on the Humanities Grant program. The date is April 12th, 2024, and the location is the College of Idaho in Caldwell.

lines.line: # Family Roots and Return to Idaho

> The speaker, Tony Ferro, recounts growing up in Treasure Valley after moving back at the age of five due to family reasons. Tony's parents and grandparents were from Idaho, which influenced their decision to return. The year of their return was 1976, marking the beginning of Tony's long-term residence in the area.

metadata.title: Family Roots and Return to Idaho
metadata.synopsis: The speaker, Tony Ferro, recounts growing up in Treasure Valley after moving back at the age of five due to family reasons. Tony's parents and grandparents were from Idaho, which influenced their decision to return. The year of their return was 1976, marking the beginning of Tony's long-term residence in the area.

lines.line: # Childhood Experiences and Family Life

> The speaker shares memories of a bustling family life filled with cousins, grandparents, and other relatives. With a father who was one of nine siblings, the speaker had a large extended family, which contributed to a lively and fun childhood. Activities such as fishing and spending time with numerous first cousins were a significant part of the speaker's early years in Idaho.

metadata.title: Childhood Experiences and Family Life
metadata.synopsis: The speaker shares memories of a bustling family life filled with cousins, grandparents, and other relatives. With a father who was one of nine siblings, the speaker had a large extended family, which contributed to a lively and fun childhood. Activities such as fishing and spending time with numerous first cousins were a significant part of the speaker's early years in Idaho.

lines.line: # Family's Religious Background and Its Influence

> The speaker delves into the family's Mormon background, discussing the impact of religion on their family dynamics and the eventual departure of many family members from the church. The speaker also touches on the poverty experienced by their grandparents and the support they received from the Mormon community, which was seen as both a blessing and a source of contention within the family.

metadata.title: Family's Religious Background and Its Influence
metadata.synopsis: The speaker delves into the family's Mormon background, discussing the impact of religion on their family dynamics and the eventual departure of many family members from the church. The speaker also touches on the poverty experienced by their grandparents and the support they received from the Mormon community, which was seen as both a blessing and a source of contention within the family.

lines.line: # High School Years and Community Connections

> The speaker reflects on their time at Caldwell High School, noting the small size of the school and the sense of community it fostered. Despite the limited size, the speaker was involved in various activities, such as tennis, debate, and swimming, which allowed them to meet students from other areas. The speaker also mentions the practice of writing letters to maintain friendships, a common method of communication before the advent of email and social media.

metadata.title: High School Years and Community Connections

Despite the limited size, the speaker was involved in various activities, such as tennis, debate, and swimming, which allowed them to meet students from other areas. The speaker also mentions the practice of writing letters to maintain friendships, a common method of communication before the advent of email and social media.

lines.line: # Choosing University of Redlands and Moving to California

> The speaker discusses their decision to attend the University of Redlands in Southern California, driven by a desire to leave Caldwell and the perception that it was a dead-end place. The speaker's mother, a Stanford alumna, encouraged them to choose a smaller school, which led to the selection of Redlands. The transition to a larger city and a new environment was surprisingly smooth for the speaker.

metadata.title: Choosing University of Redlands and Moving to California

The speaker's mother, a Stanford alumna, encouraged them to choose a smaller school, which led to the selection of Redlands. The transition to a larger city and a new environment was surprisingly smooth for the speaker.

lines.line: # Academic Pursuits and University Culture

> Initially interested in physics, the speaker chose to major in engineering, drawn to the subject's definitive answers to problems. The speaker's experience at the University of Redlands was marked by small class sizes and a close-knit campus community. Despite the challenges of a more demanding workload compared to high school, the speaker found the university culture enjoyable and intellectually stimulating.

metadata.title: Academic Pursuits and University Culture

lines.line: # Returning to Idaho and Job Search Challenges

> After graduating from university, the speaker returned to Idaho but struggled to find suitable employment in their field of engineering. This difficulty was compounded by the lack of recognition for their out-of-state degree and the limited job market in Idaho at the time. The speaker's friend CJ, who was moving to Portland, convinced them to leave Idaho again, leading to a new chapter in their life.

metadata.title: Returning to Idaho and Job Search Challenges

This difficulty was compounded by the lack of recognition for their out-of-state degree and the limited job market in Idaho at the time. The speaker's friend CJ, who was moving to Portland, convinced them to leave Idaho again, leading to a new chapter in their life.

lines.line: # Career Development and Life in Portland

> The speaker recounts their career progression, including a significant stint at Intel in Portland, where they enjoyed a comfortable lifestyle and became a homeowner. A lucrative overseas assignment in Ireland allowed the speaker to pay off student loans and invest in property. The speaker's time in Portland was characterized by a sense of freedom and a lack of concern for others' opinions on personal appearance or lifestyle.

metadata.title: Career Development and Life in Portland
metadata.synopsis: The speaker recounts their career progression, including a significant stint at Intel in Portland, where they enjoyed a comfortable lifestyle and became a homeowner. A lucrative overseas assignment in Ireland allowed the speaker to pay off student loans and invest in property. The speaker's time in Portland was characterized by a sense of freedom and a lack of concern for others' opinions on personal appearance or lifestyle.

lines.line: # Reflections on Political Engagement

> The speaker reflects on their political engagement in Portland and Seattle, contrasting it with the political climate in Idaho. They describe living in a liberal bubble in the previous cities, where meeting a Republican was a rarity. Upon returning to Idaho, the speaker's perspective shifted as they engaged with a broader range of political views and began to understand the complexities of local politics.

metadata.title: Reflections on Political Engagement

lines.line: # Graduate Studies and Union Activism at UW

> The speaker pursued graduate studies at the University of Washington, where they became actively involved in a labor union for graduate student workers. This experience was formative, providing the speaker with a deeper understanding of politics and the power dynamics between management and workers. The speaker's time in Seattle was marked by political activism and a sense of accomplishment in achieving positive changes for workers.

metadata.title: Graduate Studies and Union Activism at UW

lines.line: # Insights on Housing and Development

> The speaker shares insights on the housing market and development in Treasure Valley, criticizing the greed-driven practices of landlords and developers. They express concern over the division of communities into rich and poor neighborhoods and the lack of affordable housing options. The speaker also laments the legislative focus on less pressing issues while ignoring the housing crisis.

metadata.title: Insights on Housing and Development

They express concern over the division of communities into rich and poor neighborhoods and the lack of affordable housing options. The speaker also laments the legislative focus on less pressing issues while ignoring the housing crisis.

lines.line: # Political Candidacy and Electoral Challenges

> The speaker is currently running for state Senate, having campaigned in previous years as well. They discuss the difficulties of political engagement in Idaho, including the challenge of raising awareness among constituents about legislative issues and the actions of their representatives. The speaker emphasizes the importance of door-to-door campaigning and expresses frustration with the lack of attention to critical issues such as school privatization.

lines.line: # Advice for Newcomers to Treasure Valley

> The speaker advises newcomers to Treasure Valley to engage with their community and stay informed about local happenings. They highlight the importance of participating in city events, volunteering, and enjoying the outdoor activities that the area offers. The speaker emphasizes the need for involvement to prevent becoming isolated in an information desert and to contribute to the community's well-being.

> The speaker introduces themselves as Asher Bruce, a student conducting an oral history interview with Tony Ferro, a political candidate and native of Treasure Valley, Idaho. The interview is part of a public history class project in collaboration with the Idaho State Historical Society and is supported by the NIH Spotlight on the Humanities Grant program. The date is April 12th, 2024, and the location is the College of Idaho in Caldwell.
# Family Roots and Return to Idaho

> The speaker, Tony Ferro, recounts growing up in Treasure Valley after moving back at the age of five due to family reasons. Tony's parents and grandparents were from Idaho, which influenced their decision to return. The year of their return was 1976, marking the beginning of Tony's long-term residence in the area.
# Childhood Experiences and Family Life

> The speaker shares memories of a bustling family life filled with cousins, grandparents, and other relatives. With a father who was one of nine siblings, the speaker had a large extended family, which contributed to a lively and fun childhood. Activities such as fishing and spending time with numerous first cousins were a significant part of the speaker's early years in Idaho.
# Family's Religious Background and Its Influence

> The speaker delves into the family's Mormon background, discussing the impact of religion on their family dynamics and the eventual departure of many family members from the church. The speaker also touches on the poverty experienced by their grandparents and the support they received from the Mormon community, which was seen as both a blessing and a source of contention within the family.
# High School Years and Community Connections

> The speaker reflects on their time at Caldwell High School, noting the small size of the school and the sense of community it fostered. Despite the limited size, the speaker was involved in various activities, such as tennis, debate, and swimming, which allowed them to meet students from other areas. The speaker also mentions the practice of writing letters to maintain friendships, a common method of communication before the advent of email and social media.
# Choosing University of Redlands and Moving to California

> The speaker discusses their decision to attend the University of Redlands in Southern California, driven by a desire to leave Caldwell and the perception that it was a dead-end place. The speaker's mother, a Stanford alumna, encouraged them to choose a smaller school, which led to the selection of Redlands. The transition to a larger city and a new environment was surprisingly smooth for the speaker.
# Academic Pursuits and University Culture

> Initially interested in physics, the speaker chose to major in engineering, drawn to the subject's definitive answers to problems. The speaker's experience at the University of Redlands was marked by small class sizes and a close-knit campus community. Despite the challenges of a more demanding workload compared to high school, the speaker found the university culture enjoyable and intellectually stimulating.
# Returning to Idaho and Job Search Challenges

> After graduating from university, the speaker returned to Idaho but struggled to find suitable employment in their field of engineering. This difficulty was compounded by the lack of recognition for their out-of-state degree and the limited job market in Idaho at the time. The speaker's friend CJ, who was moving to Portland, convinced them to leave Idaho again, leading to a new chapter in their life.
# Career Development and Life in Portland

> The speaker recounts their career progression, including a significant stint at Intel in Portland, where they enjoyed a comfortable lifestyle and became a homeowner. A lucrative overseas assignment in Ireland allowed the speaker to pay off student loans and invest in property. The speaker's time in Portland was characterized by a sense of freedom and a lack of concern for others' opinions on personal appearance or lifestyle.
# Reflections on Political Engagement

> The speaker reflects on their political engagement in Portland and Seattle, contrasting it with the political climate in Idaho. They describe living in a liberal bubble in the previous cities, where meeting a Republican was a rarity. Upon returning to Idaho, the speaker's perspective shifted as they engaged with a broader range of political views and began to understand the complexities of local politics.

> The speaker pursued graduate studies at the University of Washington, where they became actively involved in a labor union for graduate student workers. This experience was formative, providing the speaker with a deeper understanding of politics and the power dynamics between management and workers. The speaker's time in Seattle was marked by political activism and a sense of accomplishment in achieving positive changes for workers.
# Insights on Housing and Development

> The speaker shares insights on the housing market and development in Treasure Valley, criticizing the greed-driven practices of landlords and developers. They express concern over the division of communities into rich and poor neighborhoods and the lack of affordable housing options. The speaker also laments the legislative focus on less pressing issues while ignoring the housing crisis.
# Political Candidacy and Electoral Challenges

> The speaker is currently running for state Senate, having campaigned in previous years as well. They discuss the difficulties of political engagement in Idaho, including the challenge of raising awareness among constituents about legislative issues and the actions of their representatives. The speaker emphasizes the importance of door-to-door campaigning and expresses frustration with the lack of attention to critical issues such as school privatization.

> The speaker advises newcomers to Treasure Valley to engage with their community and stay informed about local happenings. They highlight the importance of participating in city events, volunteering, and enjoying the outdoor activities that the area offers. The speaker emphasizes the need for involvement to prevent becoming isolated in an information desert and to contribute to the community's well-being.

The date is April 12th, 2024, and the location is the College of Idaho in Caldwell.

lines.line: # Family Roots and Childhood in Idaho

> The speaker, Tony Ferro, recounts their childhood in Idaho after moving back from Seattle at the age of five. They describe a large extended family with deep roots in the area, heavily influenced by Mormonism. Despite the family's strong Mormon background, the speaker notes that none of the children from their father's generation remained in the church, attributing this to the poverty experienced by their grandparents and the associated challenges.

metadata.title: Family Roots and Childhood in Idaho
metadata.synopsis: The speaker, Tony Ferro, recounts their childhood in Idaho after moving back from Seattle at the age of five. They describe a large extended family with deep roots in the area, heavily influenced by Mormonism. Despite the family's strong Mormon background, the speaker notes that none of the children from their father's generation remained in the church, attributing this to the poverty experienced by their grandparents and the associated challenges.

lines.line: # Early Memories and Social Dynamics

> The speaker shares early memories of growing up surrounded by a large family, with many cousins and relatives in the area. They reflect on the social dynamics within their family, noting that while their immediate family was small, the extended family was quite large. The speaker also touches on the impact of their family's religious background on their upbringing and social interactions.

metadata.title: Early Memories and Social Dynamics
metadata.synopsis: The speaker shares early memories of growing up surrounded by a large family, with many cousins and relatives in the area. They reflect on the social dynamics within their family, noting that while their immediate family was small, the extended family was quite large. The speaker also touches on the impact of their family's religious background on their upbringing and social interactions.

lines.line: # Choosing a University and Leaving Idaho

> The speaker discusses their decision to leave Idaho for college, driven by a desire to escape what they perceived as a stagnant environment in Caldwell. They chose the University of Redlands in Southern California for its small size and the opportunity it presented for a fresh start. The speaker reflects on the transition to a more urban setting and the cultural differences they encountered, as well as the challenges of adapting to the academic rigor of university life.

metadata.title: Choosing a University and Leaving Idaho

They chose the University of Redlands in Southern California for its small size and the opportunity it presented for a fresh start. The speaker reflects on the transition to a more urban setting and the cultural differences they encountered, as well as the challenges of adapting to the academic rigor of university life.

lines.line: # Career Path and Political Involvement

> After graduating from university, the speaker embarked on a professional career that included a significant tenure at Intel in Portland. They describe a period of personal and financial success, including international work experience in Ireland. The speaker also recounts their growing involvement in union activities and political engagement, which provided a deeper understanding of worker rights and the political process.

metadata.title: Career Path and Political Involvement
metadata.synopsis: After graduating from university, the speaker embarked on a professional career that included a significant tenure at Intel in Portland. They describe a period of personal and financial success, including international work experience in Ireland. The speaker also recounts their growing involvement in union activities and political engagement, which provided a deeper understanding of worker rights and the political process.

lines.line: # Returning to Idaho and Pursuing Politics

> The speaker returned to Idaho in 2018, motivated by family circumstances and a desire to be closer to relatives. They became involved in local politics, taking on a leadership role within the Democratic Party and running for state Senate. The speaker describes the challenges of political engagement in Idaho, including difficulties in recruiting candidates and informing the public about legislative issues.

metadata.title: Returning to Idaho and Pursuing Politics
metadata.synopsis: The speaker returned to Idaho in 2018, motivated by family circumstances and a desire to be closer to relatives. They became involved in local politics, taking on a leadership role within the Democratic Party and running for state Senate. The speaker describes the challenges of political engagement in Idaho, including difficulties in recruiting candidates and informing the public about legislative issues.

lines.line: # Insights into the Housing Market and Landlord Experience

> The speaker provides insights into their experience as a landlord managing rental properties in Idaho. They express frustration with the greed-driven practices of other landlords who raise rents excessively, contrasting this with their own approach of maintaining stable rents for their tenants. The speaker criticizes the lack of action on housing issues by local legislators and shares personal anecdotes that highlight the severity of the housing crisis.

metadata.title: Insights into the Housing Market and Landlord Experience

lines.line: # Advice for New Residents of Treasure Valley

> The speaker offers advice to individuals moving to the Treasure Valley, emphasizing the importance of community engagement and staying informed about local issues. They encourage newcomers to participate in city initiatives, visit the library, and explore the natural beauty of the area. The speaker also reflects on the emotional impact of the region's growth and the importance of preserving a sense of community amidst change.

story.description: The transcript features an interview with Toni Ferro, a Democratic political candidate and Treasure Valley native, conducted by Asher Bruce, a student at the College of Idaho. Topics range from Toni's family history and upbringing in the region to her higher education pursuits, professional experiences, and eventual return to Idaho for political activism. She reflects on cultural changes in Treasure Valley, differences in political climates between Idaho and other places she lived (like Portland and Seattle), and the challenges in mobilizing political and community engagement locally. Key concerns include housing affordability and political disconnects between elected officials and constituents. Toni stresses the importance of community involvement for newcomers to the area.
story.title: Ferro_Toni_OH2810 Hist305
